title: Lec-36: Carrier Sense Multiple Access/ Collision Detection | CSMA/CD | Computer Networks

segments.text: Hello friends, welcome to Gate Smashers. In today's video we are going to discuss
CSMA/CD that is Carrier Sense Multiple Access/ Collision Detection.
We had discussed about CSMA/CD in the last video.
At that time I had said that CSMA/CD is a modification of CSMA
and modification here means collision detection, means here we can also detect collision
means the node which transmits data can also detect collision.
Means let's say if I start data transmission and after some time my data collides
with some other station's data, how will I know that my data has collided, corrupt.
To find this out we use the CSMA/CD technique.
Here many times students ask that we can also use acknowledgement.
Talking about acknowledgement, let's say we have 4 stations on shared media,
and as we are using it, we use CSMA/CD in ethernet technology in LAN technique.
Here if we use acknowledgements, then the whole collision rate here will increase.
The reason is you are transmitting data and also sending acknowledgement,
so your entire media will be filled with data and acknowledgement.
Here data and data are colliding with each other,
and if you bring extra functionality of acknowledgement
then definitely collisions will increase a lot.
So the first point here is, there is no acknowledgement system.
Means the node which transmits data, let's say A starts data transmission
and after some time its data is collided. So if collision occurs,
then how will A know that my data has collided.
This is actually the funda of Collision Detection.
So here we are not using acknowledgement system, then which technology are we using?
which is that method through which A will know that its data has collided?
See like if we say here
A, B, C and D
means we have 4 stations connected to each other with a shared media.
Now let's say A starts data transmission, so what is transmission basically?
Message divided by bandwidth, transmission time.
And transmission means I have started loading bits one by one on the channel.
When bits are loaded on the channel, after that they move.
Moving means propoagation. So in propogation what happens is
all of the signal will go to B, it will go to C also and D also.
And the destination address in my header, let's say my destination address is D,
means I am sending data to D, so when B checks that destination address is D,
it will discard the data, C will also discard the data, D will accept that data.
This is actually the funda of LAN technology.
So when A starts data transmission, first it sensed, like we talked about CSMA,
so in CSMA, first it will sense, if my channel is idle, then it starts tranmssion.
So when it starts tranmssion, after some time D also starts tranmssion.
When D starts tranmssion, data of A and D will collide at a point.
Now data has collided, A is continuously sending data,
but data of A has collided, and when data collides, such a frequency is made
or the data of that signal becomes such that it has no validity, means it is useless data.
So here A doesn't know that its data has collided, it is continuously sending data
and what is the benefit of it sending data? There is no benefit.
Because the data has already collided. So here how will A know?
A will know when A is transmitting data and at the same time
collision signal comes to it.
Means I transmit data and at the same time collided data comes to me,
then A will know that I am transmitting data and collision signal comes from there,
so means my data has collided.
But if we say that transmission of A has finished means
A has transmitted data, it is idle. Some time later its collided data comes back,
now A cannot judge whether my data has collided
or some other station's data has collided.
Because at this time A is not doing transmission.
So when you are doing transmission, at the same time if your data collides
and collision signal comes back to you, only in this case you will know
that it was my data which got collided.
But if your transmission gets over, then if you receive collision signal
at that time station will not know this, station can think that
some other station's data got collided, so I am getting something else.
But actually it is possible that the same station's data has collided.
So there is only one concept of Collision Detection here that
when you are doing transmission, at the same time if you receive collision signal,
only then you will know that it was my data which got collided.
So if we see here, let's say one is station A, one is station B.
Propogation time, the propogation delay between A and B is of 1 hour.
Means what is propogation delay?
If a bit starts from here and reaches here, it will take time of 1 hour.
This is actually propogation delay. Now let's say 12 o'clock,
At 12:00PM A checks the signal, means A senses the medium,
it feels it's empty, means idle. So it immediately starts transmitting its data.
When in starts transmitting, data starts propogating, bits start moving towards B.
Now at the same time, means at 12:00PM let's say B also sensed, it will feel what?
It will also feel that channel is idle, because, I said in CSMA also, that
you can only check your own point, means if you are standing in you house,
then you can only check in front of your house if any vehicle is moving or not.
You can't check the entire street. Means B will only check here if there is a signal,
but no signal has come to B yet, so B will also transmit data.
Now what will happen? What will happen at this time?
Here after half an hour, means exactly after half an hour
both their data will be collided. Reason? This started at 12:00PM,
this started at 12:00PM, and this data of A to reach B it takes 1 hour
and data of B to reach A it takes 1 hour. What is the reason?
What is porpogation delay? Distance divided by velocity.
So here what is the distance? It is same.
Distance of A to B and B to A will be the same. And what is velocity?
Let's say if I am using fiber channel here,
then in fiber channel data moves with the speed of light, that is also constant.
When A starts transmission at 12:00PM it's bits are going one by one, and
B also starts transmission at 12:00PM, so obviously at 12:30PM,
exaclty at half an hour, data from both will collide with each other.
When both data collides, data of a new frequency which is useless data
will go in this direction and in this direction.
But when will it reach A? When will that data reach A?
When collision occurs here at 12:30PM, then think when will the data reach A?
Obviously the data will reach A after half an hour. Means at 1 o'clock,
at 1:00PM at A and at 1:00PM at B, collision signal is accepted.
Now it will know, after 1 hour, that my data has collided,
my data has collided. Means both data has come in collision.
But, the condition here is that A is doing transmission at that time,
B is doing transmission at that time, means they are transmitting till 1:00PM.
If they stop transmission before, then they will knot know.
They are still doing transmission, then they will know that
it was my data which came in collision.
So one funda can be understood from here that
transmission time should be greater than propogation delay.
This one funda can be understood. But if we talk from competitive point of view,
or if we talk from college/university level point of view,
talking about worst case here, the worst case is important. What is it?
It's the same story, A and B are connected to each other,
and propogation delay between them is 1 hour. Means 1 bit will go from here to there in 1 hour.
Now, let's say A sensed at 12:00PM and it got empty.
It immediately starts data transmission. When it starts data transmission,
one by one bits are propogated...
At this time noone is coming in between. Now suppose at 12:59:59PM
means the absolute worst case I am talking about, just before 1:00PM,
because transmission started at 12:00PM, 1 hour is transmission time,
so at 1:00PM data will reach B. And if B starts transmission at 1:00PM
then it will sense that already there is data on the channel, but
what we are taking in the worst case is at 12:59:59PM, exactly 1 second before,
B senses 1 second before, it will get what? Empty.
Why? Because the bit of A has not yet reached. It immediately transmits its data.
As soon as it transmits, its first bit collides with data of A.
B immediately knows, data reaches B instantly and B will immediately stop.
B will stop instantly, but we are taking worst case for A.
Now when collision occurs here at 12:59:59PM, collided signal is moving...
And when will it reach A? When will that collided signal reach A?
When bits have reached in 1 hour, then collided signal will also reach in 1 hour.
Means approximately at 2:00PM A will know that my data came in collision.
But under the same condition, if it is doing transmission at that time.
If it stopped transmitting at 12.05, 12.10, 12.30, 1.00, anytime before,
then it cannot judge. But if A is doing transmission at that time, and
if it is doing till 2:00PM, and at that time while doing transmission
it gets a signal of collision. Now it will judge that collision has occurred.
So worst case means how much should be my transission time?
Transission time should be gretaer than or equal to 2 times PD.
This is a very important formula, Transmission time should be gretaer than equal to 2 times PD.
You can easily know from here, this did transmission first,
its original data will reach here is 1 hour,
when collision occurs, collision signal will take 1 more hour to reach here.
So if at that time it is doing transmission,

it is a very simple concept which will be very useful to you.
So if we expand it little more, then what is transmission time?
Length of the message divided by bandwidth should be greater than equal to
